title: Agent Skills for Large Language Models: Architecture, Acquisition, Security, and the Path Forward
summary: This paper explores the shift from static language models to modular, skill-based agents that can load specific instructions and tools on demand. It provides a framework for how these agents acquire, manage, and secure new capabilities without requiring expensive model retraining.
key_findings: Agent skills represent a new abstraction layer that allows for dynamic capability extension through composable instructions and code.
A security analysis revealed that over 26% of community-contributed skills contain vulnerabilities, highlighting the need for rigorous governance.
The authors propose a four-tier, gate-based permission model to manage skill trust and lifecycle deployment.
The integration of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) is essential for standardizing how agents interact with external resources.
methodology: The authors conducted a comprehensive survey and empirical analysis of the current agent skill landscape, evaluating existing architectures, acquisition methods, and security vulnerabilities.
bias_disclaimer: AI-generated analysis may contain inaccuracies or reflect biases from the underlying model. Always perform critical evaluation.
